title: Solar Proton Event Warning
description: The orbital monitoring network reports a massive coronal mass ejection heading toward Mars. Your rover's shielding is minimal; you must decide how to shield the crew.

options.label: Deploy lead-lined blankets internally
options.log: We used our spare shielding spares to line the bunk walls, keeping the radiation dosage within acceptable limits.

options.label: Bury the rover in regolith
options.log: We spent hours digging and piling cold Mars dirt onto the hull. The crew is exhausted and dirty, but shielded.

title: Static-Charged Dust Front
description: A towering wall of red dust blocks the horizon. The triboelectric charge is throwing sparks across the cockpit glass and threatening the battery banks.

options.label: Hunker down and wait it out
options.log: We powered down the drive motors and waited. Consumed supplies while the storm raged outside.

options.label: Drive through using thermal-lidar
options.log: We pushed through the blinding dust. Static discharges damaged some sensory hull nodes, but we made distance.

title: Seized Steering Actuator
description: The front-left drive wheel shudders and locks up. Metal shavings contaminate the differential oil. You must repair it or limp along.

options.label: Replace with spare components
options.log: We performed an emergency EVA and replaced the actuator. The rover steers smoothly again.

options.label: Lock the wheel and drag it
options.log: We locked the wheel straight and dragged it. The extra friction is putting a massive drain on our power grid.

title: Abandoned Surveyor Hab
description: A defunct, half-buried inflatable habitat from the early exploratory era looms out of the dust. Its airlocks are long dead, but salvage remains.

options.label: Cycle airlock manually to scavenge
options.log: Prying open the old airlock doors took hours, but we recovered pristine structural spares and some stored water.

options.label: Blow the seal with charges
options.log: We blew the inner seal. The explosion damaged our outer bumper, but we retrieved preserved high-calorie rations and compressed O2.

title: Micro-Meteorite Hull Breach
description: A high-velocity particle punches a millimeter-wide hole through the secondary cabin seal. A high-pitched whistle warns of atmospheric decompression.

options.label: Deploy emergency sealant foam
options.log: Quick reflexes. We plugged the whistling puncture with quick-curing resin, saving our air.

options.label: Don helmets and bleed the cabin
options.log: We depressurized the main compartment to seal it mechanically. The freezing cold and lost air shook the crew.

title: Ration Discrepancy
description: A crew member discovers that someone has been double-dipping into the nutrient paste packs. Accusations fly as cabin fever sets in.

options.label: Impose strict dietary tracking
options.log: We locked the food dispensers behind passcode logs. The thief was stopped, but the atmosphere inside is hostile.

options.label: Increase rations to ease tension
options.log: We authorized a temporary calorie boost to calm everyone down. Spirits are higher, but our reserves took a hit.

title: Noctis Labyrinthus Chasm
description: Your orbital maps missed a sudden graben blocking the path. You can detour around the maze or attempt to traverse a steep, loose scree slope.

options.label: Take the long detour detour
options.log: We played it safe and bypassed the chasm. The detour added three days to our transit time.

options.label: Descend the unstable slope
options.log: We slid down the rocky scree. The suspension took a heavy beating, but we saved precious travel days.

title: RTG Thermocouple Degradation
description: The radioisotope thermoelectric generator's cooling loop is failing. Interior temperatures are dropping rapidly toward freezing.

options.label: Reroute main battery to heaters
options.log: We kept the cabin warm by pulling power directly from the main battery. Comfort comes at a steep electrical cost.

options.label: Strip insulation from cargo to patch it
options.log: We dismantled cargo bay insulation to rebuild the thermocouple shroud. The rover's shell is thinner now.

title: Crashed Cargo Drone
description: An old autonomous supply drone lies shattered in a shallow crater. Its lithium-sulfur battery pack appears intact, but unstable.

options.label: Attempt hot-wire extraction
options.log: The battery sparked violently during extraction, burning a crew member's arm, but we successfully drained its charge.

options.label: Safely dismantle for scrap
options.log: We took our time discharging the core safely. No energy gained, but we scored several solid replacement parts.

title: Condensation on Electronics
description: Moisture is condensing on the cold interior hull plates, pooling near the primary life support terminal. A short-circuit is imminent.

options.label: Crank the cabin dehumidifier
options.log: We ran the high-load atmospheric scrubbers. It reclaimed the vapor as liquid water, but drained our batteries.

options.label: Wipe down manually with rags
options.log: We spent a miserable shift hand-drying the wiring. Much of the moisture evaporated out of the cabin vents, lost forever.

title: Cosmic Ray Spike
description: A high-energy galactic cosmic ray shower hits. The crew must retreat to the heavily shielded, cramped bunk area for 36 hours.

options.label: Maintain strict, cramped isolation
options.log: Thirty-six hours of absolute confinement in the crawlspace. Tempers flared, but everyone stayed healthy.

options.label: Run essential checks during storm
options.log: We took turns in the cabin to optimize the engine's power cycle. The radiation dose required iodine treatments afterward.

title: Dust-Induced Sensor Blindness
description: Fine, iron-rich dust has coated the external optical sensors, rendering your automated pathfinding useless. You are driving blind.

options.label: Perform manual EVA cleaning
options.log: An astronaut slipped in the zero-visibility dust storm while cleaning. They cleared the sensors but sprained an ankle.

options.label: Switch to blind dead-reckoning
options.log: We navigated by gyros and wheel encoders. We clipped a hidden boulder in the dark, damaging the outer frame.

title: CO2 Scrubber Saturated
description: The primary zeolite CO2 scrubbers have saturated early. Carbon dioxide levels in the cabin are beginning to climb.

options.label: Bake out zeolite using heaters
options.log: We ran the emergency thermal cycle on the carbon scrubbers, purging the CO2 but burning a lot of electrical power.

options.label: Replace with chemical cartridges
options.log: We broke into our stockpile of consumable chemical canisters. It saved power, but diminished our repair reserve.

title: Buried Water Ice Deposit
description: A radar scan indicates a pure glacial lens of water ice just two meters below the sandy regolith. Digging will require heavy drill power.

options.label: Run the high-draw thermal drill
options.log: We ran the drill at maximum depth. We extracted pure, frozen Martian water, but the power cells are nearly flat.

options.label: Bypass to conserve rover energy
options.log: We marked the spot and drove on. We saved our batteries, but our hydration levels are dropping.

title: The Claustrophobic Breakdown
description: One of your technicians locks themselves in the storage alcove, claiming they can hear the martian wind screaming through the hull.

options.label: Administer a heavy sedative
options.log: We used our medical reserves to sedate the technician. The cabin is peaceful again, though one hand is out of commission.

options.label: Spend hours talking them down
options.log: We talked through the intercom until they calmed down. The delay cost us valuable travel time and food rations.

title: Sinking Sand Trap
description: The rover drives into a pocket of ultra-fine, dehydrated dust. The wheels spin uselessly, sinking deeper into the martian soil.

options.label: Deploy high-friction recovery tracks
options.log: We slipped tread-plates under the tires. The plates warped and broke, but we managed to crawl out of the sand trap.

options.label: Vent tire pressure to crawl out
options.log: We bled nitrogen from our tire tanks to widen the tread. We got out, but our emergency gas system is depleted.

title: Sub-Zero Battery Freeze
description: The external temperature has plunged to -110C. The primary battery compartment heater is struggling to maintain operating threshold.

options.label: Dump excess power to core warmers
options.log: We bypassed the safety relays to dump charge directly into the thermal blankets, preserving battery health.

options.label: Insulate with thermal suits
options.log: We stuffed spare thermal EVA liners into the battery casing. It worked, but the crew is furious about losing backup gear.

title: Irradiated Pioneer Lander
description: You stumble across a historical probe from the 1970s. Its radioisotope heating unit is leaking radiation, but contains rare-earth elements.

options.label: Perform quick salvage of components
options.log: We harvested the old circuit boards. The radiation dose required us to use anti-rad medication from our supply.

options.label: Keep your distance and bypass
options.log: We chose health over scraps. Driving around the hot zone cost a bit of fuel, but the crew felt respected.

title: Airlock Interlock Glitch
description: An electronic glitch prevents the inner airlock door from sealing completely during pressure equalization. Air is venting slowly.

options.label: Force the manual override crank
options.log: We sheared off the manual backup lock pin forcing the latch shut, but we successfully stopped the leak.

options.label: Perform a hot-swap of the circuit
options.log: We bypassed the electronics. The slow work allowed more cabin air to escape into the thin Martian atmosphere.

title: Induced Grid Currents
description: The planetary magnetic field lines shake from a solar impact, inducing dangerous currents in the rover's electrical grid.

options.label: Shunt excess current to ground spike
options.log: We drove a metal rod into the soil to bleed off the surge. It cost us a massive chunk of stored electricity.

options.label: Allow surge protectors to pop
options.log: The breakers blew as designed, protecting our primary battery banks but burning out two major distribution boards.

title: The Water Ration Protest
description: The crew discovers a slow leak in a greywater line. A debate erupts: should they cut personal hygiene use or drink recycled urine?

options.label: Enforce strict hygiene ban
options.log: No washing. The crew smells terrible and spirits are at an all-time low, but we preserved our drinking water.

options.label: Run aggressive purification cycles
options.log: We ran high-heat distillation to clean the contaminated graywater. It drank our battery power dry.

title: Crater Rim Route
description: A massive impact crater blocks the direct path. You can climb the steep, rocky rim or circle around the vast ejecta blanket.

options.label: Climb the steep rocky rim
options.log: We crawled up the basalt rocks. The grinding vibration shook the main cabin and drained our motors.

options.label: Circle the broad ejecta blanket
options.log: We chose the flat, easy detour. It was completely safe, but ate up two whole days of life support consumables.

title: Evaporative Cooling Leak
description: A crack in the environmental system's evaporator line causes precious water to flash-freeze and sublimate into the vacuum.

options.label: Seal the leak with vacuum tape
options.log: We patched the line quickly during an EVA. We lost some coolant water, but saved the overall thermal loop.

options.label: Bypass the external evaporator loop
options.log: We rerouted the loop internally. No water was lost, but the cabin is hot, stuffy, and the cabin fans are working overtime.

title: Crashed Orbital Drop Pod
description: A heavily charred cargo pod lies half-buried in a dune. Its tracking beacon is dead, but the internal lockers are intact.

options.label: Pry open safe with mechanical tools
options.log: Hours of grinding and leverage finally cracked the locking plates, yielding high-density survival rations.

options.label: Cut open seals with plasma torch
options.log: The plasma torch ate our spare nozzles, but we sliced through the container, finding medical kits and pressurized gas.
